behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at a module boundary (ingest/parse output handed to correlator), where service identity can accidentally leak into grouping keys. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed, (b) no AI output, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM-stage handoff. Rejected Unit because behavior text explicitly requires cross-source handoff: "regardless of which microservice emitted them." Rejected System because behavior does not require real network or subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real ingest -> parse -> correlate modules in-process with a fixed hard-case canary set containing mixed-service entries that share trace IDs. Assert one grouped request per trace ID across services and no service-based split groups.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering correctness depends on interaction between correlation and timeline/ordering logic with skew adjustment. REAL-INFRA: real OS/network timing is not required because skew is encoded in event timestamps. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks all negative. Rejected Unit because behavior text includes cross-service skew handling ("between services"), which is a handoff/order boundary, not only a single pure sort function.
behaviors.test_description: Use real correlator + timeline builder modules with synthetic cross-service events and skew cases (0ms, 500ms, 501ms canary). Verify rendered order matches expected corrected chronology for <=500ms skew and preserves deterministic tie-breaking.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is often in module wiring from metrics aggregation (baseline+p99) to anomaly reporter flag emission, not just percentile math. REAL-INFRA: no real external infra required; deterministic datasets can reproduce. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks all negative. Rejected Unit because behavior explicitly requires end result "flagged in the anomaly report," a cross-module propagation boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run aggregation and anomaly-report modules together on a fixed dataset where p99 is just below and just above 3x baseline (hard-case canaries). Assert only above-threshold windows are flagged and report payload contains the expected spike annotation.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is parser error handling propagating into pipeline control flow and skip-counter reporting. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced in-process without real files/network. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks all negative. Rejected Unit because behavior requires both non-crash pipeline behavior and reporting side effect (count), i.e., multi-module interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Execute parse -> pipeline -> reporting modules with mixed valid/malformed JSON canary inputs. Assert processing continues, malformed records are excluded from downstream correlation, and skipped-entry count matches exactly.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is throughput/backpressure behavior under real runtime scheduling, queues, and I/O characteristics; in-process fakes can hide drops/lag. MOCK-HIDE: mocked clocks/queues/transport can pass while real process falls behind. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior text is explicitly performance-under-burst ("10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind"), which depends on real system behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the built tool as a real process with a burst generator sending 10k structured logs/sec for a fixed interval; measure ingest count, processed count, queue lag, and end-to-end delay. Pass only if no loss and backlog returns to steady-state within SLO.
